tasks.title: Analise critica do ESAA atual
tasks.description: Executar uma analise critica completa do estado atual do ESAA neste repositorio. Ler os contratos canonicos em .roadmap, schemas, politicas, perfis, read models, roadmaps plugin, readme/AGENTS/CLAUDE quando existirem, implementacao do harness/runtime em src/esaa e testes em tests. Procurar falhas, bugs, inconsistencias entre configuracao, schemas, comandos CLI, harness/orchestrator, documentacao e comportamento observado. Validar comandos essenciais com PYTHONPATH=src quando seguro. Ao final produzir um relatorio completo em docs/qa/ESAA-current-critical-analysis.md contendo escopo, metodologia, evidencias, achados priorizados, riscos, inconsistencias, bugs potenciais, pontos fortes, insights arquiteturais, recomendacoes e proximas tarefas sugeridas. Nao editar .roadmap manualmente; qualquer conclusao deve ser evidence-based.

checks: behavioral reproductions for atomicity, review lock and plugin dispatch completed

tasks.title: Governed admin bootstrap for contract edits
tasks.description: Specify the one-time governance bootstrap required before tasks in this roadmap may change canonical contract files under .roadmap. The spec must define an explicit admin/orchestrator path that allows only .roadmap/AGENT_CONTRACT.yaml, .roadmap/ORCHESTRATOR_CONTRACT.yaml, .roadmap/agent_result.schema.json, .roadmap/roadmap.schema.json and required PARCER profiles. It must explicitly forbid writes to .roadmap/activity.jsonl, .roadmap/roadmap.json, .roadmap/issues.json, .roadmap/lessons.json, backups, snapshots and arbitrary .roadmap paths. Include acceptance criteria proving that the bootstrap is narrow, temporary, auditable and does not bypass append-only event-store governance.

tasks.title: Verify governed admin bootstrap boundaries
tasks.description: Verify that BOOT-1804 defines a safe bootstrap path for contract edits without permitting direct event-store or read-model mutation. The QA report must enumerate allowed and forbidden paths, confirm that activity.jsonl and projections remain single-writer/derived artifacts, and state whether the later .roadmap contract edits in this roadmap are executable only through the declared bootstrap. No source code implementation is required in this task.

tasks.title: Serializable append lock policy
tasks.description: Specify the serializable append model for the event store. The policy must move from byte-level append locking to a transaction that covers parse, project, validate expected event sequence, assign final event_seq/event_id values, append events and save projections. Define expected inputs such as expected_first_seq and expected_projection_hash, stale-state rejection behavior, retry guidance, and invariants: no duplicate event_seq, no gaps, no stale projection write, and no direct mutation outside the Orchestrator path.

tasks.title: Implement serializable append transaction
tasks.description: Implement the serializable append policy from FIX-1806. Add a transactional store API that acquires the activity lock before deciding final event_seq values, rereads activity.jsonl inside the lock, validates the expected sequence and projection hash, assigns contiguous event ids, appends events, materializes read models and writes projections under the same critical section. Refactor service paths that currently compute event_seq before append so they use this API. Preserve existing command outputs and structured ESAAError codes for stale state and lock timeout.

tasks.title: Verify serializable append transaction
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence for the serializable append transaction. Cover two concurrent processes attempting to append from the same starting state, stale expected sequence rejection, projection hash revalidation, lock timeout behavior, no duplicate event_seq, no gaps and verify_status=ok after successful concurrent operations. The QA report must include command output summaries and explain why byte-level append locking alone was insufficient.

tasks.title: Atomic complete and file effects policy
tasks.description: Specify atomic handling of complete plus file effects. The policy must guarantee that final files are never written unless the corresponding complete and orchestrator.file.write events have been admitted. Define staging, validation, transactional append, final atomic rename/application, recovery behavior after interruption, cleanup of abandoned staged artifacts, and acceptance criteria for append failure, lock timeout and partial-write prevention.

tasks.title: Implement atomic complete and file effects
tasks.description: Implement the atomic complete/file effect policy from FIX-1805 using the serializable transaction API from FIX-1806-IMPL. Stage file_update contents outside final paths, validate boundaries and write conflicts before admission, append complete and orchestrator.file.write events transactionally, then apply final filesystem changes only after the event-store append succeeds. Ensure append failure, stale sequence and lock timeout leave no final output file behind.

tasks.title: Verify atomic complete and file effects
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence proving that file side effects cannot appear without admitted ESAA events. Cover lock timeout before append, stale expected sequence, simulated append failure, successful complete with file write, staged artifact cleanup and verify_status=ok. The key regression assertion is that a final docs/src/tests output path does not exist when complete returns STORE_LOCK_TIMEOUT or stale-state error.

tasks.title: File effect artifact and hash audit policy
tasks.description: Specify forensic audit metadata for file effects. The policy must extend orchestrator.file.write to record path, before_sha256, after_sha256, bytes, encoding, artifact_sha256 and artifact_path. Define the content-addressed artifact format under .roadmap/artifacts/file-effects/<sha256>.json, including content or patch payload, canonical hash inputs, missing-artifact behavior, replay verification and retention rules. This task depends on atomic file effects because artifacts must describe admitted effects only.

tasks.title: Implement file effect artifacts and hashes
tasks.description: Implement the file effect artifact policy from FIX-1808. Generate content-addressed artifact records before final application, compute before/after hashes and byte counts, include artifact metadata in orchestrator.file.write events, and expose helper validation for replay/audit. Ensure artifact writes are coordinated with the atomic complete transaction so an artifact cannot claim an effect that was not admitted.

tasks.title: Verify file effect artifact auditability
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence for file effect artifact auditability. Cover successful artifact creation, before/after hash correctness, bytes and encoding fields, missing artifact detection, hash mismatch detection, replay/audit helper output, and verify_status=ok after normal writes. The QA report must show an example orchestrator.file.write payload with the new metadata.

tasks.title: Independent QA review role policy
tasks.description: Specify the review role policy that aligns runtime behavior with the ESAA contract. complete must remain restricted to the actor that claimed the task, but review must be authorized by QA role or policy rather than by the completion owner. Define actor-role resolution, default QA actors, expected rejection code for non-QA reviewers, compatibility impact on existing tests and docs, and the exact contract/doc updates needed after the BOOT-1804 bootstrap.

tasks.title: Implement independent QA review policy
tasks.description: Implement independent QA review. Refactor projector/service validation so review approval no longer requires assigned_to ownership, while complete still enforces assigned_to == actor. Add role resolution for agent-qa through agents_swarm or runtime policy. Update CLI examples and contract files allowed by the bootstrap so docs and runtime agree. Existing owner-review tests must be changed to assert rejection unless the owner also has QA role.

tasks.title: Verify independent QA review policy
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence for independent QA review. Cover agent-spec claim/complete followed by agent-qa review approve, owner review rejected when owner lacks QA role, request_changes by agent-qa returns to in_progress, complete by non-owner still rejected, and documentation examples matching the runtime. The QA report must include before/after behavior for the previous LOCK_VIOLATION conflict.

tasks.title: Unified eligible and run plugin dispatch policy
tasks.description: Specify one canonical eligibility view shared by eligible and run. The spec must require run to consume tasks_with_planned_plugins or equivalent, admit plugin tasks through deterministic task.create before claim, preserve event-store authority after admission, respect dependencies and parallel_groups, and report source=roadmap_plugin before admission and source=event_store after admission.

tasks.title: Implement unified eligible and run plugin dispatch
tasks.description: Refactor run so it uses the same planned-plugin task view as eligible. Before claiming a plugin-only task, append a deterministic task.create admission event using the same validation path as claim_task. Ensure run --parallel still groups by planned write sets, handles dependencies, and does not silently prefer seed/event-store tasks over lexicographically earlier plugin tasks.

tasks.title: Verify run consumes roadmap plugin tasks
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence proving eligible and run use the same plugin task universe. Cover a plugin task listed by eligible and then claimed by run, deterministic task.create admission before claim, dependency blocking across plugin and event-store tasks, parallel wave grouping, and verify_status=ok after run.

tasks.title: Done status evidence for issue.report
tasks.description: Specify how issue.report preserves the real status when invoked on a done task. The preferred design is to allow prior_status=done only for issue.report; if schema compatibility requires otherwise, define an observed_task_status field with const done. The spec must remove the current done-to-review masking behavior, update schema/contract wording, and define validation rules so claim/complete/review over done remain forbidden.

tasks.title: Implement done status evidence for issue.report
tasks.description: Implement the FIX-1810 schema and runtime behavior. Update agent_result.schema.json and validation so issue.report can preserve done status evidence, remove service.report_issue masking from done to review, keep claim/complete/review over done rejected, and update docs/contracts under the BOOT-1804 allowlist. Ensure existing issue.report flows in todo, in_progress and review remain valid.

tasks.title: Verify issue.report preserves done status
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence for issue.report on done tasks. Cover done task issue.report payload preserving done status, claim/complete/review rejected on done, schema rejection for invalid prior_status/action combinations, existing non-done issue.report compatibility, and verify_status=ok after the issue event.

tasks.title: Hotfix create validation policy
tasks.description: Specify validation for hotfix.create. The policy must require an existing open issue, a fixes target that references an existing task, and for immutable-done hotfixes the original task must be done. Define structured error codes for issue missing, issue not open, fixes task missing, fixes task not done, duplicate hotfix and invalid scope_patch. Preserve the existing valid flow issue.report -> hotfix.create -> claim -> complete -> review -> issue.resolve.

tasks.title: Implement hotfix create validation
tasks.description: Implement the hotfix.create validation policy from FIX-1811 in service/build_hotfix_event paths. Validate issue existence and open status from projected issues, validate fixes task existence and done status when required, reject duplicate hotfixes as before, validate scope_patch is non-empty and normalized, and return short structured ESAAError codes suitable for CLI output.

tasks.title: Verify hotfix create validation
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence for hotfix.create validation. Cover issue nonexistent, issue already resolved, fixes task nonexistent, fixes task not done, empty/invalid scope_patch, duplicate hotfix and a valid hotfix for an open issue on a done task. The QA report must include the exact error codes and show the valid flow still reaches verify_status=ok.

tasks.title: runner.metrics reserved action contract
tasks.description: Specify runner.metrics as a reserved Orchestrator action. The spec must define its authority boundary, minimum payload fields or reference to runner_metrics.py, relationship to external runners such as Claude Code, Codex and Antigravity, and the documents/contracts that must list it: AGENT_CONTRACT, ORCHESTRATOR_CONTRACT, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, readme and constants. The action must remain forbidden for agent outputs.

tasks.title: Align contracts with runner.metrics
tasks.description: Update canonical contracts and operational documents so runner.metrics is listed as a reserved Orchestrator action everywhere the reserved action vocabulary appears. Keep it out of allowed_agent_actions and agent_result.schema.json action enum. Update the contract consistency checker so missing runner.metrics is reported as drift.

tasks.title: Verify runner.metrics contract alignment
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence that runner.metrics is consistently reserved to the Orchestrator. Cover contract checker failure when runner.metrics is removed from one canonical source, success when all sources align, agent output rejection if runner.metrics is attempted as an agent action, and existing runner metrics CLI behavior remaining valid.

tasks.title: Baseline lessons reseed policy
tasks.description: Specify how baseline lessons LES-0001, LES-0002 and LES-0003 are reconstructed after init and activity clear. The preferred design is to reseed them with an event that materialize can replay, using orchestrator.view.mutate with a lessons payload if no new action is needed. Define canonical lesson ids, titles, rules, scopes, enforcement modes, source refs and acceptance criteria for dispatch-context injection after reset.

tasks.title: Implement baseline lessons reseed
tasks.description: Implement baseline lessons reseed through replayable events. Add a canonical baseline lesson builder, make init and activity clear append or otherwise materialize the baseline lessons through the approved event path, and ensure dispatch-context includes active LES-0001, LES-0002 and LES-0003 after reset. Do not hand-edit lessons.json. Update documents that previously described active lessons as static if needed.

tasks.title: Verify baseline lessons reseed
tasks.description: Create tests and QA evidence for baseline lessons reseed. Cover init, activity clear, project/replay, lessons.json content, dispatch-context lesson injection for applicable task kinds, no manual read-model edits, and verify_status=ok. Confirm LES-0001, LES-0002 and LES-0003 survive deterministic projection.

tasks.title: Critical findings audit coverage specification
tasks.description: Specify expanded audit coverage for all critical findings from docs/qa/ESAA-current-critical-analysis.md. The criteria must cover atomicity of file effects, serializable event-store locking, independent QA review policy, run/eligible plugin parity, issue.report on done preserving status, hotfix.create validation, runner.metrics contract alignment and baseline lessons reseed. Define expected finding ids, severity levels and evidence fields for each checker.

tasks.title: Implement critical findings audit coverage
tasks.description: Expand src/audit checkers or add a new checker so the audit suite detects every finding specified by AUD-1814. The implementation must inspect contracts, schemas, runtime code patterns and tests where appropriate. It must detect missing runner.metrics in reserved actions, review role drift, absence of baseline lesson reseed, hotfix validation gaps, plugin dispatch parity gaps, file effect audit metadata gaps and non-serializable append patterns.

tasks.title: Verify critical findings audit coverage
tasks.description: Create final QA tests and report for the critical findings audit suite. Run the full pytest suite, all audit scripts, roadmap schema validation and esaa verify. Add regression fixtures proving each audit finding is detected when a source is intentionally missing or inconsistent. The QA report must summarize final command outputs and confirm that the new roadmap's original findings are now covered by automated audit checks.

tasks.title: Levantamento critico do sistema ESAA atual
tasks.description: Executar um levantamento critico do estado atual do ESAA apos o ciclo de critical fixes. Validar contratos, schemas, event store, read models, CLI, harness/runtime, auditorias, testes e documentacao. Procurar falhas, bugs e inconsistencias entre configuracao, harness e comportamento observado. Produzir relatorio evidence-based em docs/qa/ESAA-current-system-survey.md com resumo executivo, metodologia, evidencias, achados priorizados, riscos residuais, insights e recomendacoes.

checks: verify inicial ok e auditorias executadas
achados reproduzidos com dry-run para plugin run, hotfix e lessons

tasks.title: Atomic complete file effects wired into harness
tasks.description: Corrigir o primeiro achado critico do levantamento QA-1815: integrar staging atomico de file_updates ao caminho real do harness em submit/run. O complete com file_updates nao pode criar arquivo final sem evento admitido; o evento orchestrator.file.write deve carregar metadata minima de efeito com hashes/artifact para permitir auditoria. Incluir testes de regressao end-to-end para falha de append e payload de efeito.

checks: pytest targeted file effects/run tests: 38 passed

tasks.title: Wire serializable append into harness
tasks.description: Corrigir CRIT-02 do levantamento QA-1815: o lock serializavel append_transactional existe em src/esaa/store.py, mas os fluxos governados do harness ainda usam append_events + save projections fora da secao critica. Refatorar _commit_orchestrator_events, submit e run para usar append_transactional com expected_first_seq/projection_hash quando aplicavel, preservando staging atomico de file_updates. Incluir testes end-to-end provando que submit/run nao dependem mais de append_events e que a projecao final permanece ok.

tasks.title: Definir critérios de auditoria de consistência entre contratos e documentos operacionais
tasks.description: Especificar a checklist de verificação cruzada entre AGENT_CONTRACT.yaml, ORCHESTRATOR_CONTRACT.yaml, agent_result.schema.json, os PARCER_PROFILE.*.yaml, .claude/CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md e .roadmap/init.yaml. Critérios devem cobrir: drift de versão (declarações 0.4.1 vs schemas const 0.4.0), divergência de vocabulário de ações, regras de prior_status, boundaries por task_kind, e modelo de invocação two-step. Saída: lista enumerada de invariantes verificáveis com fonte canônica de cada uma.

checks: AUD-1000: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Definir critérios de conformidade de schema e drift de versão
tasks.description: Especificar como validar roadmap.json, roadmap.*.json (plugins), issues.json e lessons.json contra seus respectivos *.schema.json, e como detectar drift entre schema_version declarado nos dados (const 0.4.0), esaa_version e as versões 0.4.1 declaradas nos contratos. Incluir verificação de meta.run (projection_hash_sha256 formato, verify_status enum) e dos índices by_status/by_kind.

checks: AUD-1100: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Definir critérios de integridade do event store e das projeções (runtime)
tasks.description: Especificar as verificações de runtime sobre .roadmap/activity.jsonl e os read models: append-only, event_seq monotônico sem gaps, ordenação, derivabilidade determinística das projeções a partir do event store, e coerência entre o estado efetivo (roadmap + eventos) e roadmap.json. Incluir o caso activity_future_templates.jsonl (não deve contaminar o estado efetivo).

checks: AUD-1200: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Definir critérios de auditoria do runtime: dispatch two-step e workflow gates
tasks.description: Especificar como auditar o comportamento de runtime definido em ORCHESTRATOR_CONTRACT.yaml e RUNTIME_POLICY.yaml: modelo two-step (claim/complete por status), os gates WG-001..WG-005 e seus reject_codes, transições da state_machine, imutabilidade de done, limites de tentativa (max_attempts, cooldown, TTL) e o pipeline determinístico. Incluir verificação de que toda violação mapeada tem reject_code e gate correspondentes.

checks: AUD-1300: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Definir critérios de rastreabilidade lessons ↔ gates ↔ reject_codes e consolidação final
tasks.description: Especificar a verificação de rastreabilidade bidirecional entre lessons.json (status=active, enforcement.mode), os workflow_gate_references e os reject_codes, garantindo que cada lesson de enforcement reject está ligada a um gate e que nenhum gate referencia lesson inexistente. Especificar também o formato do relatório consolidado de inconsistências do ESAA agregando AUD-1002, AUD-1102, AUD-1202 e AUD-1302.

checks: AUD-1400: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: External runner telemetry contract
tasks.description: Especificar a telemetria real de execucao para runners externos (Claude Code, Codex, Antigravity ou outro executor que abre o projeto e usa a CLI do esaa-core). A especificacao deve deixar claro que adapters nativos Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini sao opcionais; o requisito obrigatorio e registrar metricas do ciclo executado pelo runner externo sem quebrar o modelo single-writer. Definir campos minimos: task_id, actor, runner_id, runner_kind, model, command_surface, started_at, ended_at, latency_ms, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cost_estimate, status, error_code opcional e correlation_id. Definir como essas metricas entram no event store por acao reservada do Orchestrator ou por comando deterministico validado, como aparecem em esaa metrics, como lidar com valores desconhecidos (null, nunca inventar), e quais invariantes devem ser preservadas: append-only, event_seq monotonic, nenhuma edicao manual de read models e nenhuma dependencia de provider API nativo.

checks: CMM-1700: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Production hotfix trace scenario
tasks.description: Especificar um cenario demonstravel de hotfix que apareca como trilha real de eventos ESAA, nao apenas como teste unitario. O fluxo minimo deve ser: issue.report -> hotfix.create -> claim -> complete com pelo menos 2 verification.checks -> review approve -> issue.resolve -> verify.ok. Definir se o cenario roda em fixture temporaria, workspace real ou ambos; quando rodar no workspace real, deve usar apenas comandos oficiais do esaa-core e nunca edicao manual de activity.jsonl. Definir dados canonicos do issue, nome do hotfix task, scope_patch permitido, arquivo de patch seguro em src/hotfix/ ou fixture equivalente, criterios de aceite e evidencias esperadas em metrics/eligible/verify. Explicitar que a tarefa done original permanece imutavel e que o issue.resolve e indispensavel para fechar a projecao de issues.

checks: CMM-1710: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Concurrent write conflict policy
tasks.description: Especificar a politica completa de conflito para execucao concorrente com parallel_groups. A especificacao deve definir write_set planejado (outputs.files da tarefa), write_set efetivo (file_updates.path do complete), conflito por igualdade de arquivo e por prefixo quando um output representa diretorio, e comportamento esperado antes e depois do dispatch. Definir regras: tarefas com write_set planejado sobreposto nao entram no mesmo parallel_group; completes concorrentes sao serializados no append; se o write_set efetivo conflitar com evento ja admitido na mesma wave, o Orchestrator deve rejeitar ou reserializar conforme politica declarada; nenhum arquivo pode ser escrito antes de validacao de boundary e conflito. Incluir exemplos com duas tarefas escrevendo o mesmo arquivo, diretorios distintos, diretorio pai/filho e hotfix scope_patch.

checks: CMM-1720: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Snapshot compaction operational policy
tasks.description: Especificar a politica operacional de snapshot e compactacao do event store. A especificacao deve definir quando snapshotar, como nomear snapshots, o que entra no checkpoint (roadmap, issues, lessons, last_event_seq, projection_hash, created_at, source hash), como arquivar eventos antigos, como provar que replay a partir de snapshot + eventos remanescentes produz o mesmo hash, e como restaurar em caso de corrupcao. Definir modos seguros: snapshot somente leitura; compactacao com arquivo de archive preservado; dry-run obrigatorio; recusa quando verify_status != ok; e regra de nunca compactar eventos acima do ultimo verify.ok confirmado. Incluir criterio de retencao e invariantes de auditoria para nao destruir a trilha forense.

checks: CMM-1730: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Protocol vocabulary evolution
tasks.description: Especificar a evolucao formal do vocabulario entre o artigo/arXiv v0.3, o estudo clinic-asr e o core v0.4.1. A especificacao deve explicar por que aparecem promote, phase.complete, backlog e ready no artigo, enquanto o core atual usa todo, in_progress, review, done e actions claim, complete, review, issue.report. Definir se promote/phase.complete sao historicos, perfil alternativo phase-gated ou extensoes opcionais. Mapear cada termo antigo para o equivalente atual ou declarar que pertence a outro profile. O objetivo e remover ambiguidade publica sem reabrir tarefas done nem quebrar compatibilidade. Incluir tabela de migracao, exemplos de projection e recomendacao de nomenclatura para futuras versoes do paper/readme.

checks: CMM-1740: conteudo produzido ou preservado conforme output declarado

tasks.title: Implementar harness de auditoria do dispatch two-step e dos workflow gates

tasks.title: External runner telemetry implementation
tasks.description: Implementar a telemetria especificada em CMM-1700 no runtime local. Adicionar um modulo dedicado para normalizar metricas de runners externos, validar tipos e calcular duracoes quando started_at/ended_at estiverem presentes. Expor comando CLI deterministico para registrar metricas de dispatch/runner sem chamar LLM diretamente, por exemplo esaa runner metrics ou comando equivalente coerente com a CLI atual. Atualizar compute_metrics para agregar latencia total, tokens totais, contagens por runner_kind/model/status e falhas por error_code. Garantir que metricas desconhecidas sejam preservadas como null ou omitidas de agregados numericos, sem estimativas inventadas. A implementacao deve manter o Orchestrator como unico writer, append_events como caminho unico de persistencia, e nao deve exigir adapters nativos de provedores.

tasks.title: Production hotfix trace tooling
tasks.description: Implementar o suporte operacional para executar o cenario de hotfix descrito em CMM-1710 de forma reproduzivel. Criar um comando ou helper deterministico que monte os envelopes e comandos oficiais do ciclo, com modo padrao seguro em workspace temporario e modo explicito para admitir a demonstracao no workspace atual quando solicitado pelo operador. O helper deve produzir resumo JSON com event_seq inicial/final, issue_id, hotfix_task_id, eventos encontrados, verify_status final e caminhos de arquivos tocados. Nao deve escrever diretamente no event store; deve chamar os mesmos metodos de service/CLI usados por issue report, hotfix create, claim, complete, review e issue resolve.

tasks.title: Concurrent write conflict enforcement
tasks.description: Implementar a politica de conflito de CMM-1720 no selecionador de parallel_groups e no caminho de admissao de complete. Adicionar funcoes puras para normalizar write sets, detectar conflitos por arquivo exato e prefixo diretorio, explicar o conflito em formato JSON e impedir que tarefas conflitantes sejam despachadas na mesma wave. No caminho de complete, validar file_updates.path contra writes ja admitidos na wave atual antes de aplicar efeitos. Se houver conflito, emitir output.rejected com codigo claro (por exemplo WRITE_CONFLICT) e sem side effects de arquivo. Preservar append-only, lock multi-processo existente, boundaries por task_kind e scope_patch de hotfix.

tasks.title: Snapshot compaction implementation
tasks.description: Implementar a compactacao operacional definida em CMM-1730. Estender o modulo de snapshot e a CLI para suportar dry-run, archive de eventos compactados, manifesto de compactacao, validacao de verify_status ok antes da operacao e replay a partir de checkpoint. A implementacao deve manter uma forma auditavel de recuperar a historia: nenhum evento deve ser apagado sem archive e manifest; o snapshot deve carregar last_event_seq e projection_hash; o verify deve conseguir comparar estado reconstruido por snapshot + tail events. Incluir erros estruturados para tentativa de compactar sem verify ok, before maior que ultimo verify.ok, snapshot ausente ou hash divergente.

Registrar status de git limpo para arquivos gerados por testes.

tasks.title: Behavioral critical audit contract
tasks.description: Especificar a correcao do achado MED-02: critical_findings retorna zero findings mesmo quando probes comportamentais reproduzem bugs. Definir checks que criem fixtures temporarias e validem comportamento real para atomicidade, hotfix, prior_status done, activity clear, plugin run, review policy, dry-run e runner metrics.

tasks.title: File effect artifact retention policy
tasks.description: Especificar a correcao dos achados MED-05 e LOW-02: artifacts de file effects existem, mas a politica de retencao/versionamento/backfill ainda nao esta definida e 34 eventos historicos nao possuem effects. Definir se artifacts sao canonicos, versionados, arquivados ou derivados, e como lidar com eventos antigos.

tasks.title: Done evidence quality policy
tasks.description: Especificar mitigacao do achado MED-06: tarefas podem chegar a done com evidencia formal fraca quando executadas em lote. Definir criterios de evidencia forte por task_kind, checks verificaveis, relatorio de done sem teste associado, regra de review independente para trilhas criticas e como marcar fechamento administrativo sem confundir com implementacao semantica completa.

tasks.title: Implement behavioral critical audit
tasks.description: Atualizar src/audit/critical_findings.py para executar probes comportamentais seguros em temp dirs, nao apenas procurar strings. O checker deve reportar findings se hotfix orfao for aceito, se issue report done gravar review, se clear remover lessons, se run nao consumir plugin late, se QA independente nao estiver ativa ou se file effects nao forem recuperaveis.

tasks.title: Implement artifact retention and audit tooling
tasks.description: Implementar comandos/auditoria para verificar artifacts referenciados por orchestrator.file.write, detectar artifact ausente ou hash divergente, listar eventos historicos sem effects, e opcionalmente gerar backfill auditavel quando o conteudo puder ser reconstruido. Nao inventar conteudo historico sem evidencia.

tasks.title: Implement done evidence quality checks
tasks.description: Implementar auditoria ou comando que identifique tarefas done com evidencia fraca: verification checks genericos, outputs inexistentes, ausencia de teste ou doc QA, complete/review pelo mesmo actor quando policy exige independencia, e file_updates preservados sem mudanca relevante. O resultado deve ser informativo e nao reabrir done automaticamente.

tasks.title: Verify behavioral critical audit
tasks.description: Criar testes que introduzem fixtures boas e ruins para critical_findings. Confirmar que o auditor retorna zero em estado corrigido e findings especificos quando um comportamento e quebrado por monkeypatch/fixture. Rodar o auditor contra o repo atual no final e registrar evidencia.

checks: critical and traceability audits returned zero findings

tasks.title: Verify artifact retention and audit
tasks.description: Criar testes para artifact presente, ausente, hash divergente, evento antigo sem effects, e politica de gitignore/versionamento. Confirmar que o auditor sinaliza corretamente sem quebrar replay de eventos historicos aceitos.

checks: critical and traceability audits returned zero findings

tasks.title: Verify done evidence quality checks
tasks.description: Criar fixtures com tarefa done forte e tarefa done fraca. Confirmar que o auditor classifica corretamente sem gerar falsos positivos para hotfixes validos, docs-only specs e QA tasks. Registrar evidencia da execucao contra o event store atual.

checks: critical and traceability audits returned zero findings
